visualAssessment: Iter5 screenshots confirm the DEV.OS aesthetic. Typography is clearly mono-first (IBM Plex Mono body/HUD) with Oxanium used for display headlines and giant section numbers — no trace of generic Inter/Geist. The hot-amber #FF7A1A accent reads strongly as a signature color against the cool blue/purple/cyan palette: it carries the section numbers (02/03/04/06 visible), the SEND SIGNAL CTA, the form borders, the HUD progress bar, and the timeline bullets. Skills iter5 is the cleanest example of the new spec: giant amber '03' sits far left, the 3D hologram docks left, and the HUD window (not glass) sits right with 'Frontend' in display, skill bars in mono, and sub-slide dots bottom-right exactly as designed. Contact shows the terminal-style form with amber-bordered NAME_/EMAIL_/PAYLOAD_ inputs, mono labels, and an amber filled SEND SIGNAL button. Projects shows EXECUTION.LAYER headline stacked with the laptop docking top-center and glyph-orbit visible. Experience renders a dashed-bordered timeline entry (Sr. Fullstack Engineer · Aurora Labs) with amber period tag and highlight bullets. About shows the asymmetric right-docked laptop plus the 3-col stats row (08 / 47 / +09). Hero capture lands mid-boundary between hero exit and about entrance, but within it the boot-log terminal panel, amber eyebrow timestamp, and the stacked SYSTEM / // BOOT headline fragments are all present. Laptop recognizability: keyboard instanced grid is visible in about + projects captures; screen code-editor face reads as a colored horizontal bar-stack from the wide angles the dock uses, and the hologram projector slab adds the blue floor glow. Dock variance across sections is clearly different — the 3D is on the right in hero/about, off-screen left in skills, top-lifted in projects, right in experience, and center-small in contact. Text and 3D occupy distinct halves; the scrim gradients keep text readable where they overlap. HUD frame with corner brackets, edge ticks, and the top-left SectionHUD progress bar are visible across all captures.
warnings: Hero iter5 screenshot captures the hero-to-about boundary (hero is min-h-screen with no scrub pin, so scrolling +600px lands inside about content). The boot-log and eyebrow timestamp are still visible, but IDENTITY.INIT headline from the next section shares the frame.
Experience iter5 has the giant '05' scrolled just above the viewport (top-right offset + 600px scroll). The timeline entry renders cleanly.
